questions: DraggableImage.tsx currently disables ALL gallery drag — was drag disabled for a reason (e.g., conflicting with @dnd-kit sort in ShotImageManager)? Re-enabling it globally could break shot image sorting. Should we gate drag-enable to only when GenerationsPane is over the video editor, or is re-enabling globally safe?

For variant drops: should the asset file reference be the variant's Supabase storage URL directly, or should we copy/re-upload the file into the timeline-assets bucket? Direct reference is simpler but couples the asset to the generation's storage lifecycle (if generation is deleted, asset breaks). Re-upload is safer but duplicates storage.

The back bar — should it show just timeline name + back button, or also include playback controls / render button / save status? The current FullEditorLayout has these in the preview panel area already, so minimal back bar seems right, but want to confirm.

For the assets panel drop target (Step 6): the current AssetPanel has drag-out support but no drop-in handler. Adding a drop zone changes its interaction model. Should the entire panel be a drop target, or should there be an explicit 'drop here' zone?
success_criteria: Video editor page uses full viewport height — no GlobalHeader visible, no EditorPane dots, no pane-margin padding
Navigating away from /tools/video-editor restores normal layout (GlobalHeader, panes, margins)
A minimal back bar (~32-40px) shows at top of editor with navigation link and timeline name
Generation variants can be dragged from GenerationsPane and dropped onto the timeline to create clips
Generation variants can be dragged onto the assets panel to register without creating a clip
Dropped variant assets include generationId and variantId in their AssetRegistryEntry
Existing drag flows (asset-key from panel, file upload) continue working unchanged
Assets panel in PropertiesPanel is collapsed by default
Assets with generationId/variantId show a click-through action that opens MediaLightbox to the source generation+variant
No hardcoded color classes (zinc-*, gray-*, slate-*) remain in video editor components — all use theme tokens
Editor visually matches Reigh's dark theme and feels like a native tool
Compact mode in EditorPane still works on non-editor routes
npm run build passes with no type errors
assumptions: Re-enabling drag on DraggableImage is safe if we scope it properly (e.g., only set generation-specific dataTransfer types that the shot timeline ignores)
Variant assets will reference the original storage URL directly (no re-upload) for simplicity — if the generation is deleted, the asset will show a broken state, which is acceptable for v1
The back bar is minimal: just a back link + timeline name. Save/render status stays in the existing preview/chrome areas
The entire AssetPanel surface becomes a drop target (with visual drop indicator), since it's a natural target and avoids adding a separate zone
MediaLightbox can be rendered from within the video editor component tree without conflicting with other lightbox instances
The timeline list view (no timeline selected) does not need full-screen treatment — only the active editor shell does
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
